children.heading: Approval of projects
children.label: a Approval of projects
children.text: The President, acting through the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency, may approve projects under the alternative procedures adopted under this section for any major disaster or emergency declared on or after . The Administrator may also apply the alternate procedures adopted under this section to a major disaster or emergency declared before enactment of this Act for which construction has not begun as of the date of enactment of this Act.January 29, 20131See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Adoption
children.label: b Adoption
children.text: The Administrator, in coordination with States, tribal and local governments, and owners or operators of private nonprofit facilities, may adopt alternative procedures to administer assistance provided under sections 5170b(a)(3)(A), 5172, 5173, and 5192(a)(5) of this title.
reducing the costs to the Federal Government of providing such assistance;
increasing flexibility in the administration of such assistance;
expediting the provision of such assistance to a State, tribal or local government, or owner or operator of a private nonprofit facility; and

providing financial incentives and disincentives for a State, tribal or local government, or owner or operator of a private nonprofit facility for the timely and cost-effective completion of projects with such assistance.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Participation in the alternative procedures adopted under this section shall be at the election of a State, tribal or local government, or owner or operator of a private nonprofit facility consistent with procedures determined by the Administrator.

children.heading: No conditions
children.label: 2 No conditions
children.text: The President may not condition the provision of Federal assistance under this chapter on the election by a State, local, or Indian tribal government, or owner or operator of a private nonprofit facility to participate in the alternative procedures adopted under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: making grants on the basis of fixed estimates, if the State, tribal or local government, or owner or operator of the private nonprofit facility agrees to be responsible for any actual costs that exceed the estimate;

children.label: i
children.text: the cost of repair, restoration, reconstruction, or replacement of a public facility owned or controlled by the State, tribal or local government or owner or operator of a private nonprofit facility; and

children.label: ii
children.text: management expenses;

children.label: C
children.text: consolidating, to the extent determined appropriate by the Administrator, the facilities of a State, tribal or local government, or owner or operator of a private nonprofit facility as a single project based upon the estimates adopted under the procedures;

children.label: i
children.text: cost-effective activities that reduce the risk of future damage, hardship, or suffering from a major disaster; and

children.label: ii
children.text: other activities to improve future Public Assistance operations or planning;

children.label: E
children.text: in determining eligible costs under , the Administrator shall make available, at an applicant’s request and where the Administrator or the certified cost estimate prepared by the applicant’s professionally licensed engineers has estimated an eligible Federal share for a project of at least $5,000,000, an independent expert panel to validate the estimated eligible cost consistent with applicable regulations and policies implementing this section;section 5172 of this title

children.label: F
children.text: in determining eligible costs under , the Administrator shall, at the applicant’s request, consider properly conducted and certified cost estimates prepared by professionally licensed engineers (mutually agreed upon by the Administrator and the applicant), to the extent that such estimates comply with applicable regulations, policy, and guidance; andsection 5172 of this title

children.label: G
children.text: once certified by a professionally licensed engineer and accepted by the Administrator, the estimates on which grants made pursuant to this section are based shall be presumed to be reasonable and eligible costs, as long as there is no evidence of fraud.

children.label: A
children.text: making grants on the basis of fixed estimates to provide financial incentives and disincentives for the timely or cost-effective completion if the State, tribal or local government, or owner or operator of the private nonprofit facility agrees to be responsible to pay for any actual costs that exceed the estimate;

children.label: B
children.text: using a sliding scale for determining the Federal share for removal of debris and wreckage based on the time it takes to complete debris and wreckage removal;

children.label: C
children.text: allowing use of program income from recycled debris without offset to the grant amount;

children.label: D
children.text: reimbursing base and overtime wages for employees and extra hires of a State, tribal or local government, or owner or operator of a private nonprofit facility performing or administering debris and wreckage removal;

children.label: E
children.text: providing incentives to a State or tribal or local government to have a debris management plan approved by the Administrator and have pre-qualified 1 or more debris and wreckage removal contractors before the date of declaration of the major disaster; and

children.label: i
children.text: debris management planning;

children.label: ii
children.text: acquisition of debris management equipment for current or future use; and

children.label: iii
children.text: other activities to improve future debris removal operations, as determined by the Administrator.
waive notice and comment rulemaking, if the Administrator determines the waiver is necessary to expeditiously implement this section; and
carry out the alternative procedures under this section as a pilot program.

children.heading: Overtime payments
children.label: g Overtime payments
children.text: The guidelines for reimbursement for costs under subsection (e)(2)(D) shall ensure that no State or local government is denied reimbursement for overtime payments that are required pursuant to the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 ( et seq.).29 U.S.C. 201

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not earlier than 3 years, and not later than 5 years, after , the Inspector General of the Department of Homeland Security shall submit to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives a report on the alternative procedures for the repair, restoration, and replacement of damaged facilities under authorized under this section.January 29, 2013section 5172 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: whether the alternative procedures helped to improve the general speed of disaster recovery;

children.label: B
children.text: the accuracy of the estimates relied upon;

children.label: C
children.text: whether the financial incentives and disincentives were effective;

children.label: D
children.text: whether the alternative procedures were cost effective;

children.label: E
children.text: whether the independent expert panel described in subsection (e)(1)(E) was effective; and

children.label: F
children.text: recommendations for whether the alternative procedures should be continued and any recommendations for changes to the alternative procedures.
